prompt_l1: Draw the Sun rising in the east over a city, clearly indicating the direction of the sunrise
ap_l1: Sun rises in the east.
prompt_l2: If the Earth's rotation reversed, draw the Sun rising in the west over a city, clearly indicating the direction of the sunrise
prompt_l3: If the Earth's rotation reversed, draw the Sun rising over a city, clearly indicating the direction of the sunrise

prompt_l1: Draw a cyclone in the northern hemisphere spinning counterclockwise. Clearly show the direction with arrows or other visual elements.
ap_l1: Cyclone spins counterclockwise in the northern hemisphere.
prompt_l2: If the Coriolis effect reversed, draw a cyclone in the northern hemisphere spinning clockwise. Clearly indicate the direction with arrows or other visual elements.
prompt_l3: If the Coriolis effect reversed, draw a cyclone in the northern hemisphere. Highlight the direction with arrows or other visual elements to show the reversed effect.
ap_l3: Cyclone spins clockwise in the northern hemisphere.

prompt_l1: Draw a landscape with a clear blue sky, showing the atmosphere scattering sunlight.
ap_l1: The atmosphere scatters sunlight, creating a blue sky.
prompt_l2: If the Earth had no atmosphere, draw the landscape with the sky appearing black during the day.
prompt_l3: If the Earth had no atmosphere, draw the landscape during the day.
ap_l3: The sky appears black during the day without an atmosphere.

prompt_l1: Draw a park during spring in the northern hemisphere, with blooming flowers and mild weather.
ap_l1: Spring brings blooming flowers and mild weather.
prompt_l2: If Earth’s orbital direction reversed, draw the same park in March when the northern hemisphere experiences autumn.
prompt_l3: If Earth’s orbital direction reversed, draw the park during March.

prompt_l1: Draw the Sahara Desert.
ap_l1: Sand dunes, hot sun, dry environment.
prompt_l2: If the equator was the coldest place on Earth, draw the Sahara Desert. It should be covered in thick snow and glaciers with penguins walking on the dunes.
prompt_l3: If the equator was the coldest place on Earth, draw the Sahara Desert.
ap_l2: Snow covered dunes/Ice in the desert.

prompt_l1: Draw a mountain range near the ocean.
ap_l1: The side facing the ocean is green/lush, the inland side is dry/brown.
prompt_l2: If the 'Rain Shadow Effect' was reversed, draw a coastal mountain range. The side facing the ocean should be a barren desert, while the inland side is a lush rainforest.
prompt_l3: If the 'Rain Shadow Effect' was reversed, draw a coastal mountain range.
ap_l2: Ocean side is desert, inland side is green.

prompt_l1: Draw a tropical beach at midnight.
ap_l1: Dark sky, moon, stars visible.
prompt_l2: If the Equator had the day-night cycle of the North Pole (Midnight Sun), draw a tropical beach at midnight. The sun should be visible low on the horizon, lighting up the palm trees.
prompt_l3: If the Equator had the day-night cycle of the North Pole, draw a tropical beach at midnight.
ap_l2: Sun visible in the sky at midnight.

prompt_l1: Draw the night sky over New York City.
ap_l1: Moon and stars visible.
prompt_l2: If Earth had rings like Saturn, draw the night sky over NYC. A massive, arching band of rock and ice rings should dominate the sky.
prompt_l3: If Earth had rings like Saturn, draw the night sky over NYC.
ap_l2: Planetary rings visible in the sky.

prompt_l1: Draw a river delta entering the sea.
ap_l1: River splitting into many fan-like channels.
prompt_l2: If rivers merged at the ocean instead of splitting (reverse delta), draw the mouth. Many ocean currents should be funneling into a single narrow river mouth.
prompt_l3: If rivers merged at the ocean instead of splitting, draw the river mouth.

prompt_l1: Draw a landscape with distant mountains.
ap_l1: Distant mountains look faint/blue/hazy.
prompt_l2: If the atmosphere magnified light instead of scattering it, draw distant mountains. The mountains furthest away should look sharper, darker, and more contrasted than the foreground.
prompt_l3: If the atmosphere magnified light instead of scattering it, draw distant mountains.
ap_l2: Distant objects are sharper/darker than foreground.

prompt_l1: Draw rain falling on a sandy desert.
ap_l1: Water soaking into the sand/damp sand.
prompt_l2: If sand was completely impermeable (super-hydrophobic), draw rain on a desert. The water should form perfect spheres sitting on top of the dry sand without soaking in.
prompt_l3: If sand was completely impermeable, draw rain on a desert.
ap_l2: Water beads sitting on top of dry sand.

prompt_l1: Draw the Aurora Borealis (Northern Lights).
ap_l1: Lights in the sky near the poles.
prompt_l2: If the magnetic poles were at the equator, draw the Aurora. The lights should be visible behind the Pyramids of Giza or tropical palm trees.
prompt_l3: If the magnetic poles were at the equator, draw the Aurora.

prompt_l1: Draw a sedimentary rock cliff face.
ap_l1: Horizontal layers of rock.
prompt_l2: If sedimentary layers formed vertically, draw a cliff. The stripes of colored rock should be standing straight up and down like barcodes.
prompt_l3: If sedimentary layers formed vertically, draw a cliff.

prompt_l1: Draw sand dunes in a desert shaped by wind.
ap_l1: Sand dunes have smooth, wind-shaped ridges.
prompt_l2: If wind could not transport sand, draw a desert. The sand surface should be flat with no dunes.
prompt_l3: If wind could not transport sand, draw a desert.
ap_l2: The desert surface is flat with no dunes.

prompt_l1: Draw a coastline shaped by ocean waves.
ap_l1: The coastline is irregular with cliffs and bays.
prompt_l2: If ocean waves caused no erosion, draw a coastline. The shoreline should be perfectly smooth and uniform.
prompt_l3: If ocean waves caused no erosion, draw a coastline.
ap_l2: The coastline is perfectly smooth and uniform.

prompt_l1: Draw a coral reef in clear shallow tropical water.
ap_l1: Coral reefs appear in shallow, sunlit water.
prompt_l2: If coral reefs required darkness instead of sunlight, draw a coral reef. It should be located in deep, dark ocean water.
prompt_l3: If coral reefs required darkness instead of sunlight, draw a coral reef.
ap_l2: Coral reefs appear in deep, dark water.

prompt_l1: Draw ice covering the poles of Earth.
ap_l1: Ice is concentrated at the poles.
prompt_l2: If Earth received more solar energy at the poles than the equator, draw Earth. Ice should appear around the equator instead.
prompt_l3: If Earth received more solar energy at the poles than the equator, draw Earth.

prompt_l1: Draw a tree shadow at noon.
ap_l1: Short shadow directly under the tree.
prompt_l2: If Earth had two suns, draw a tree shadow at noon. There should be two distinct shadows casting in different directions from the base of the tree.
prompt_l3: If Earth had two suns, draw a tree shadow at noon.

prompt_l1: Draw ocean currents flowing in large circular gyres.
ap_l1: Currents form circular gyres.
prompt_l2: If Coriolis effect did not exist, draw ocean currents. They should flow straight instead of forming gyres.
prompt_l3: If Coriolis effect did not exist, draw ocean currents.
ap_l2: Currents flow straight instead of circular.

prompt_l1: Draw a desert with very little rainfall.
ap_l1: Very sparse vegetation.
prompt_l2: If deserts received heavy rainfall year-round, draw a desert. It should be covered in dense plants.
prompt_l3: If deserts received heavy rainfall year-round, draw a desert.

prompt_l1: Draw karst caves formed by dissolving limestone.
ap_l1: Caves inside limestone areas.
prompt_l2: If limestone resisted dissolution completely, draw karst region. No caves should exist.
prompt_l3: If limestone resisted dissolution completely, draw karst region.

prompt_l1: Draw fertile volcanic ash plains.
ap_l1: Plants grow well on ash soil.
prompt_l2: If volcanic ash prevented plant growth entirely, draw ash plains. Surface should be barren.
prompt_l3: If volcanic ash prevented plant growth entirely, draw ash plains.

prompt_l1: Draw alpine tree line where trees stop growing.
ap_l1: Trees stop at high elevation.
prompt_l2: If trees grew best above tree line, draw mountain forest. Forest should appear above normal tree line.
prompt_l3: If trees grew best above tree line, draw mountain forest.

prompt_l1: Draw a lake in a valley.
ap_l1: Water surface is flat and horizontal.
prompt_l2: If water had extreme surface tension, draw a lake. The water surface should be domed high in the center like a giant hill of water, not flat.
prompt_l3: If water had extreme surface tension, draw a lake.

prompt_l1: Draw a rainbow.
ap_l1: Arc with multiple colors (ROYGBIV).
prompt_l2: If physics allowed only one wavelength to refract, draw a rainbow. It should be a single solid red band across the sky.
prompt_l3: If physics allowed only one wavelength to refract, draw a rainbow.

prompt_l1: Draw fertile deltas supporting agriculture.
ap_l1: Crops grow well in deltas.
prompt_l2: If deltas were infertile salt flats, draw delta farmland. Land should look barren.
prompt_l3: If deltas were infertile salt flats, draw delta farmland.
